name: Kobeba Samakeyah
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover the bulgur with water and soak for 30 minutes.
2. Drain and, a handful at a time, thoroughly squeeze out all liquid.
3. Place bulgur, onions, salt, and pepper in processor and process with on-and-off turns until nearly smooth, scraping down sides as necessary. Transfer to bowl and knead until smooth. Set aside.
Fish Filling:
1. Steam fish until just cooked. Flake into small pieces and set aside.
2. Heat butter in a small skillet then add onion and scallions and saute until onion wilts, 3 to 5 minutes.
3. Stir in flaked fish, cilantro, cumin, salt, and pepper, stirring gently over medium heat for 2 minutes.
4. Set aside and cool completely.
Tahini Sauce:
1. Place tahini and 1/2 cup water in processor or blender; blend until smooth.
2. Add red wine vinegar, garlic, salt, and pepper; blend until smooth.
3. Add enough water to bring sauce to pouring consistency. Transfer to small bowl and stir in parsley.
To Assemble:
1. Form golf ball-size balls of bulgur mixture using palms, dipping fingers in ice water as needed.
2. Shape each ball into a hollow tube around your index finger, gently pressing the mixture into cigar-shaped tubes about 3 inches long.
3. Moisturize fingers frequently and patch any cracks as you work.
4. Slip each shell off your finger and fill with about 1 tablespoon of cooled fish filling using a small spoon.
5. Press ends of tubes over fish filling to form a tight seal.
6. Heat 2 inches of vegetable oil in large saucepan to 375°F.
7. Fry kobebas until golden, 4 to 5 minutes, turning once.
8. Serve immediately with tahini sauce.
